finding_info.desc: Define and maintain role-based access control, through determining and documenting the access rights necessary for each role within the enterprise to successfully carry out its assigned duties. Perform access control reviews of enterprise assets to validate that all privileges are authorized, on a recurring schedule at a minimum annually, or more frequently.

checks.desc: **IAM Access Analyzer** presence and status are evaluated per account and Region. An analyzer in `ACTIVE` state indicates continuous analysis of supported resources and IAM activity to identify external, internal, and unused access.
checks.name: IAM Access Analyzer is enabled

message: Login to the AWS Console. Choose your account name on the top right of the window -> My Account -> Contact Information.
status_detail: Login to the AWS Console. Choose your account name on the top right of the window -> My Account -> Contact Information.

finding_info.desc: Establish and maintain contact information for parties that need to be informed of security incidents. Contacts may include internal staff, service providers, law enforcement, cyber insurance providers, relevant government agencies, Information Sharing and Analysis Center (ISAC) partners, or other stakeholders. Verify contacts annually to ensure that information is up-to-date.

checks.desc: **AWS account contact information** is current for the **primary contact** and the **alternate contacts** for `security`, `billing`, and `operations`, with accurate email addresses and phone numbers.
checks.name: AWS account contact information is current

compliance.control: Establish and maintain contact information for parties that need to be informed of security incidents. Contacts may include internal staff, service providers, law enforcement, cyber insurance providers, relevant government agencies, Information Sharing and Analysis Center (ISAC) partners, or other stakeholders. Verify contacts annually to ensure that information is up-to-date.

message: SECURITY, BILLING and OPERATIONS contacts not found or they are not different between each other and between ROOT contact.
status_detail: SECURITY, BILLING and OPERATIONS contacts not found or they are not different between each other and between ROOT contact.

finding_info.desc: Establish and maintain contact information for parties that need to be informed of security incidents. Contacts may include internal staff, service providers, law enforcement, cyber insurance providers, relevant government agencies, Information Sharing and Analysis Center (ISAC) partners, or other stakeholders. Verify contacts annually to ensure that information is up-to-date.

checks.desc: **AWS account alternate contacts** are defined for **Security**, **Billing**, and **Operations** with `name`, `email`, and `phone`. The finding evaluates that all three exist, are distinct from one another, and differ from the **primary (root) contact**.
checks.name: AWS account has distinct Security, Billing, and Operations contact details, different from each other and from the root contact

compliance.control: Establish and maintain contact information for parties that need to be informed of security incidents. message: No Backup Vault exist.
status_detail: No Backup Vault exist.

finding_info.desc: Perform automated backups of in-scope enterprise assets. Run backups weekly, or more frequently, based on the sensitivity of the data.

checks.name: At least one AWS Backup vault exists

compliance.control: Perform automated backups of in-scope enterprise assets. Run backups weekly, or more frequently, based on the sensitivity of the data.

message: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 viewers can use HTTP or HTTPS.
status_detail: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 viewers can use HTTP or HTTPS.

finding_info.desc: Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH).

checks.desc: CloudFront distributions require viewer connections over **HTTPS** when the default cache behavior `viewer_protocol_policy` is `https-only` or `redirect-to-https`. Configurations that use `allow-all` permit HTTP.
checks.name: CloudFront distribution has viewer protocol policy set to HTTPS only or redirect to HTTPS

compliance.control: Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH).

message: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 has logging disabled.
status_detail: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 has logging disabled.

finding_info.desc: Collect audit logs. Ensure that logging, per the enterprise's audit log management process, has been enabled across enterprise assets.

checks.desc:
**CloudFront distributions** record viewer requests using **standard access logs** (S3), **real-time log configurations**, or **Standard Logging v2** via CloudWatch Logs delivery sources.

The finding evaluates whether at least one logging mechanism is active so request metadata is captured for each distribution.
checks.name: CloudFront distribution has logging enabled

compliance.control: Collect audit logs. Ensure that logging, per the enterprise's audit log management process, has been enabled across enterprise assets.

message: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 does encrypt traffic to custom origins.
status_detail: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 does encrypt traffic to custom origins.

finding_info.desc: Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH).

checks.desc: **CloudFront distributions** are evaluated for **TLS to origins**. The check ensures custom origins use `origin_protocol_policy`=`https-only`, or `match-viewer` only when the viewer protocol policy disallows HTTP. For S3 origins, it inspects the viewer protocol policy and flags `allow-all` as permitting non-encrypted paths.
checks.name: CloudFront distribution encrypts traffic to custom origins

compliance.control: Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH).

message: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 is not using a deprecated SSL protocol.
status_detail: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 is not using a deprecated SSL protocol.

finding_info.desc: Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH).

checks.desc: CloudFront distributions have origins whose `OriginSslProtocols` allow **deprecated SSL/TLS versions** (`SSLv3`, `TLSv1`, `TLSv1.1`) for CloudFront-to-origin HTTPS connections.
checks.name: CloudFront distribution does not use SSLv3, TLSv1, or TLSv1.1 for origin connections

compliance.control: Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH).

message: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 is not using AWS WAF web ACL.
status_detail: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 is not using AWS WAF web ACL.

finding_info.desc: Perform application layer filtering. Example implementations include a filtering proxy, application layer firewall, or gateway.

checks.desc:
**CloudFront distributions** are assessed for an associated **AWS WAF** web ACL that inspects and filters HTTP/S requests at the edge.

The finding highlights distributions without this web ACL association.
checks.name: CloudFront distribution uses an AWS WAF web ACL

compliance.control: Perform application layer filtering. Example implementations include a filtering proxy, application layer firewall, or gateway.

message: No CloudTrail trails are configured to log Amazon Bedrock API calls.
status_detail: No CloudTrail trails are configured to log Amazon Bedrock API calls.

finding_info.desc: Collect audit logs. Ensure that logging, per the enterprise's audit log management process, has been enabled across enterprise assets.

checks.desc:
**At least one actively logging CloudTrail trail** records **Amazon Bedrock API activity** through management events or advanced event selectors targeting Bedrock resources.

This check covers **control-plane** operations such as configuration changes through CloudTrail management events and can also cover **data-plane** Bedrock events when advanced event selectors target Bedrock resource types.
checks.name: CloudTrail logs Amazon Bedrock API calls for security auditing

compliance.control: Collect audit logs. Ensure that logging, per the enterprise's audit log management process, has been enabled across enterprise assets.

message: AWS Config has no Organization Aggregator configured in any region (delegated administrator status could not be determined).

finding_info.desc: Centralize, to the extent possible, audit log collection and retention across enterprise assets in accordance with the documented audit log management process. Example implementations primarily include leveraging a SIEM tool to centralize multiple log sources.

checks.desc: **AWS Config** has a delegated administrator registered via AWS Organizations and at least one Configuration Aggregator with an OrganizationAggregationSource that covers all AWS regions, ensuring centralized org-wide configuration visibility.
checks.name: AWS Config has a delegated administrator and an organization aggregator covering all AWS regions

compliance.control: Centralize, to the extent possible, audit log collection and retention across enterprise assets in accordance with the documented audit log management process. Example implementations primarily include leveraging a SIEM tool to centralize multiple log sources.

message: AWS Config recorder 162665602291 is disabled.
status_detail: AWS Config recorder 162665602291 is disabled.

requirement_attributes.section: 1. Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets

finding_info.desc: Establish and maintain an accurate, detailed, and up-to-date inventory of all enterprise assets with the potential to store or process data, to include: end-user devices (including portable and mobile), network devices, non-computing/IoT devices, and servers. Ensure the inventory records the network address (if static), hardware address, machine name, enterprise asset owner, department for each asset, and whether the asset has been approved to connect to the network. For mobile end-user devices, MDM type tools can support this process, where appropriate. This inventory includes assets connected to the infrastructure physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments. Additionally, it includes assets that are regularly connected to the enterprise's network infrastructure, even if they are not under control of the enterprise. Review and update the inventory of all enterprise assets bi-annually, or more frequently.

checks.desc: **AWS accounts** have **AWS Config recorders** active and healthy in each Region. It identifies Regions with no recorder, a disabled recorder, or a recorder in a failure state.
checks.name: AWS Config recorder is enabled and not in failure state or disabled

message: AWS Config recorder 162665602291 is disabled.
status_detail: AWS Config recorder 162665602291 is disabled.

requirement_attributes.section: 1. Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets

finding_info.desc: Establish and maintain an accurate, detailed, and up-to-date inventory of all enterprise assets with the potential to store or process data, to include: end-user devices (including portable and mobile), network devices, non-computing/IoT devices, and servers. Ensure the inventory records the network address (if static), hardware address, machine name, enterprise asset owner, department for each asset, and whether the asset has been approved to connect to the network. For mobile end-user devices, MDM type tools can support this process, where appropriate. This inventory includes assets connected to the infrastructure physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments. Additionally, it includes assets that are regularly connected to the enterprise's network infrastructure, even if they are not under control of the enterprise. Review and update the inventory of all enterprise assets bi-annually, or more frequently.

message: DynamoDB table Testtable is not protected by a backup plan.
status_detail: DynamoDB table Testtable is not protected by a backup plan.

finding_info.desc: Perform automated backups of in-scope enterprise assets. Run backups weekly, or more frequently, based on the sensitivity of the data.

checks.desc:
**DynamoDB tables** are evaluated for inclusion in an **AWS Backup backup plan** through resource assignments, including explicit tables, resource-type wildcards, or all-resources coverage.

The result indicates whether a table is governed by scheduled backups and retention defined by the plan.
checks.name: DynamoDB table is protected by a backup plan

message: DynamoDB table Testtable is using DEFAULT encryption.
status_detail: DynamoDB table Testtable is using DEFAULT encryption.

finding_info.desc: Encrypt sensitive data at rest on servers, applications, and databases. Storage-layer encryption, also known as server-side encryption, meets the minimum requirement of this Safeguard. Additional encryption methods may include application-layer encryption, also known as client-side encryption, where access to the data storage device(s) does not permit access to the plain-text data.

checks.desc: **DynamoDB tables** use **AWS KMS keys** (`KMS`) for encryption at rest instead of the default service-owned key
checks.name: DynamoDB table is encrypted at rest with AWS KMS

message: EMR Account has Block Public Access enabled.
status_detail: EMR Account has Block Public Access enabled.

finding_info.desc: Design and maintain a secure network architecture. A secure network architecture must address segmentation, least privilege, and availability, at a minimum. Example implementations may include documentation, policy, and design components.

checks.desc: Amazon EMR account-level **Block Public Access** configuration is assessed per Region. When `BlockPublicSecurityGroupRules` is enabled, clusters cannot use security groups that allow inbound public sources (`0.0.0.0/0`, `::/0`) except on permitted ports.
checks.name: EMR account has Block Public Access enabled

finding_info.desc: Design and maintain a secure network architecture. A secure network architecture must address segmentation, least privilege, and availability, at a minimum. Example implementations may include documentation, policy, and design components.

checks.desc: Amazon EMR account-level **Block Public Access** configuration is assessed per Region. When `BlockPublicSecurityGroupRules` is enabled, clusters cannot use security groups that allow inbound public sources (`0.0.0.0/0`, `::/0`) except on permitted ports.
checks.name: EMR account has Block Public Access enabled

compliance.control: Design and maintain a secure network architecture. A secure network architecture must address segmentation, least privilege, and availability, at a minimum. Example implementations may include documentation, policy, and design components.

message: EventBridge event bus default does not allow cross-account access.

finding_info.desc: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

checks.desc:

message: Root user in the account was last accessed 0 days ago.
status_detail: Root user in the account was last accessed 0 days ago.

requirement_attributes.section: 4. Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

finding_info.desc: Manage default accounts on enterprise assets and software, such as root, administrator, and other pre-configured vendor accounts. Example implementations can include: disabling default accounts or making them unusable.

checks.desc: **AWS IAM root user** activity is assessed by inspecting `last-used` timestamps for the root password and access keys. The finding indicates when the root identity has been used recently for console or programmatic access.
checks.name: AWS account root user has not been used in the last day

compliance.control: Manage default accounts on enterprise assets and software, such as root, administrator, and other pre-configured vendor accounts. Example implementations can include: disabling default accounts or making them unusable.

message: AWS policy AWSLambdaDynamoDBExecutionRole is attached but does not allow '*:*' administrative privileges.
status_detail: AWS policy AWSLambdaDynamoDBExecutionRole is attached but does not allow '*:*' administrative privileges.

finding_info.desc: Restrict administrator privileges to dedicated administrator accounts on enterprise assets. Conduct general computing activities, such as internet browsing, email, and productivity suite use, from the user's primary, non-privileged account.

checks.desc: **IAM AWS-managed policies** attached to identities are inspected for statements that allow `Action:'*'` on `Resource:'*'`-i.e., full administrative `*:*` permissions
checks.name: Attached AWS-managed IAM policy does not allow '*:*' administrative privileges

compliance.control: Restrict administrator privileges to dedicated administrator accounts on enterprise assets. Conduct general computing activities, such as internet browsing, email, and productivity suite use, from the user's primary, non-privileged account.

message: No Resource Explorer Indexes found.
status_detail: No Resource Explorer Indexes found.

requirement_attributes.section: 1. Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets

finding_info.desc: Establish and maintain an accurate, detailed, and up-to-date inventory of all enterprise assets with the potential to store or process data, to include: end-user devices (including portable and mobile), network devices, non-computing/IoT devices, and servers. Ensure the inventory records the network address (if static), hardware address, machine name, enterprise asset owner, department for each asset, and whether the asset has been approved to connect to the network. For mobile end-user devices, MDM type tools can support this process, where appropriate. This inventory includes assets connected to the infrastructure physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments. Additionally, it includes assets that are regularly connected to the enterprise's network infrastructure, even if they are not under control of the enterprise. Review and update the inventory of all enterprise assets bi-annually, or more frequently.

checks.desc: **AWS Resource Explorer** has user-owned **indexes** present in the account. The assessment determines whether at least one index exists in any enabled Region for resource cataloging and search.
checks.name: Resource Explorer indexes exist

compliance.control: Establish and maintain an accurate, detailed, and up-to-date inventory of all enterprise assets with the potential to store or process data, to include: end-user devices (including portable and mobile), network devices, non-computing/IoT devices, and servers. Ensure the inventory records the network address (if static), hardware address, machine name, enterprise asset owner, department for each asset, and whether the asset has been approved to connect to the network. For mobile end-user devices, MDM type tools can support this process, where appropriate. This inventory includes assets connected to the infrastructure physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments. Additionally, it includes assets that are regularly connected to the enterprise's network infrastructure, even if they are not under control of the enterprise. Review and update the inventory of all enterprise assets bi-annually, or more frequently.

finding_info.desc: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

checks.desc: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **Object Ownership** set to `BucketOwnerEnforced`, which disables bucket and object ACLs. Buckets using any other ownership setting indicate that ACLs remain enabled.

message: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb does not have a bucket policy.
status_detail: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb does not have a bucket policy.

finding_info.desc: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

checks.desc:
**Amazon S3 bucket policies** are analyzed for statements that grant **cross-account access**.

Any policy that names principals outside the owning account (other account IDs or `Principal: "*"`) is treated as cross-account; absence of a policy implies no cross-account grants.
checks.name: S3 bucket policy does not allow cross-account access

compliance.control: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

message: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb does not have correct cross region replication configuration.
status_detail: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb does not have correct cross region replication configuration.

finding_info.desc: Establish and maintain an isolated instance of recovery data. Example implementations include, version controlling backup destinations through offline, cloud, or off-site systems or services.

checks.desc:
**Amazon S3 buckets** use **cross-Region replication** with `versioning` and an enabled rule that targets a destination bucket in a different AWS Region.

Buckets with same-Region targets, missing destinations, or disabled `versioning` don't meet this replication posture.
checks.name: S3 bucket has cross-region replication configured to a bucket in a different region

compliance.control: Establish and maintain an isolated instance of recovery data. Example implementations include, version controlling backup destinations through offline, cloud, or off-site systems or services.

message: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb has Server Side Encryption with AES256.
status_detail: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb has Server Side Encryption with AES256.

finding_info.desc: Encrypt sensitive data at rest on servers, applications, and databases. Storage-layer encryption, also known as server-side encryption, meets the minimum requirement of this Safeguard. Additional encryption methods may include application-layer encryption, also known as client-side encryption, where access to the data storage device(s) does not permit access to the plain-text data.

checks.desc: [DEPRECATED] **Amazon S3 buckets** have a default **server-side encryption** setting that automatically encrypts new objects using `SSE-S3` or `SSE-KMS`. This evaluates whether a bucket has a default encryption configuration defined.
checks.name: [DEPRECATED] S3 bucket has default server-side encryption (SSE) enabled

compliance.control: Encrypt sensitive data at rest on servers, applications, and databases. Storage-layer encryption, also known as server-side encryption, meets the minimum requirement of this Safeguard. Additional encryption methods may include application-layer encryption, also known as client-side encryption, where access to the data storage device(s) does not permit access to the plain-text data.

message: Server Side Encryption is not configured with kms for S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb.
status_detail: Server Side Encryption is not configured with kms for S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb.

finding_info.desc: Encrypt sensitive data at rest on servers, applications, and databases. Storage-layer encryption, also known as server-side encryption, meets the minimum requirement of this Safeguard. Additional encryption methods may include application-layer encryption, also known as client-side encryption, where access to the data storage device(s) does not permit access to the plain-text data.

checks.desc: **Amazon S3 buckets** use server-side encryption with **AWS KMS** keys, including dual-layer `aws:kms:dsse`. The evaluation identifies buckets whose default encryption is `aws:kms` or `aws:kms:dsse` rather than SSE-S3.
checks.name: S3 bucket has server-side encryption with AWS KMS

compliance.control: Encrypt sensitive data at rest on servers, applications, and databases. Storage-layer encryption, also known as server-side encryption, meets the minimum requirement of this Safeguard. Additional encryption methods may include application-layer encryption, also known as client-side encryption, where access to the data storage device(s) does not permit access to the plain-text data.

compliance.control: Retain data according to the enterprise's documented data management process. Data retention must include both minimum and maximum timelines.

message: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb does not have a bucket policy.

finding_info.desc: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

checks.desc: **Amazon S3 bucket policies** are evaluated for **public write permissions** (e.g., `s3:PutObject`, `s3:Delete*`, or `s3:*`). Account or bucket **Public Access Block** that restricts public buckets is considered when determining exposure.
checks.name: S3 bucket policy does not allow public write access

compliance.control: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

message: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb is not public.
status_detail: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb is not public.

finding_info.desc: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

checks.desc: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **public access** via ACLs and bucket policies. The check identifies account or bucket `PublicAccessBlock` protections (`IgnorePublicAcls`, `RestrictPublicBuckets`) and flags buckets granting group access to `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers`, or with a public bucket policy.
checks.name: S3 bucket is not publicly accessible to Everyone or Authenticated Users

compliance.control: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

message: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb is not publicly listable.
status_detail: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb is not publicly listable.

finding_info.desc: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

checks.desc: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **public listing via ACLs**. Grants of `READ`, `READ_ACP`, or `FULL_CONTROL` to the `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers` groups are identified. Effective **Block Public Access** at account or bucket level (notably `IgnorePublicAcls` and `RestrictPublicBuckets`) is considered in the evaluation.
checks.name: S3 bucket is not publicly listable by Everyone or any authenticated AWS user

compliance.control: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

message: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb is not publicly writable.
status_detail: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb is not publicly writable.

finding_info.desc: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

checks.desc: **Amazon S3 buckets** are assessed for ACL grants that allow **public write** access to `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers` via `WRITE`, `WRITE_ACP`, or `FULL_CONTROL`. Effective **Block Public Access** at account or bucket level (`ignore_public_acls`, `restrict_public_buckets`) is considered.
checks.name: S3 bucket ACL does not grant write access to Everyone or any AWS customer

compliance.control: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

message: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb does not have a bucket policy, thus it allows HTTP requests.
status_detail: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb does not have a bucket policy, thus it allows HTTP requests.

finding_info.desc: Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH).

checks.desc:
**Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for a bucket policy that enforces **secure transport** by denying requests when `aws:SecureTransport` is `false`.

Buckets without this explicit denial, or without a policy, are treated as allowing access over insecure transport.
checks.name: S3 bucket policy denies requests over insecure transport

compliance.control: Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH).

message: S3 Bucket aegis-demo-model-artifacts-3a4c37eb has server access logging disabled.

finding_info.desc: Collect audit logs. Ensure that logging, per the enterprise's audit log management process, has been enabled across enterprise assets.

checks.desc: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **server access logging** configured to record access requests and deliver logs to a designated destination bucket.
checks.name: S3 bucket has server access logging enabled

compliance.control: Collect audit logs. Ensure that logging, per the enterprise's audit log management process, has been enabled across enterprise assets.

requirement_attributes.section: 2. Inventory and Control of Software Assets

finding_info.desc: Establish and maintain a detailed inventory of all licensed software installed on enterprise assets. The software inventory must document the title, publisher, initial install/use date, and business purpose for each entry; where appropriate, include the Uniform Resource Locator (URL), app store(s), version(s), deployment mechanism, decommission date, and number of licenses. Review and update the software inventory bi-annually, or more frequently.

compliance.control: Establish and maintain a detailed inventory of all licensed software installed on enterprise assets. The software inventory must document the title, publisher, initial install/use date, and business purpose for each entry; where appropriate, include the Uniform Resource Locator (URL), app store(s), version(s), deployment mechanism, decommission date, and number of licenses. Review and update the software inventory bi-annually, or more frequently.

finding_info.desc: Enforce automatic device lockout following a predetermined threshold of local failed authentication attempts on portable end-user devices, where supported. Example implementations include Microsoft® InTune Device Lock and Apple® Configuration Profile maxFailedAttempts.

finding_info.desc: Establish and maintain a process to accept and address reports of software vulnerabilities, including providing a means for external entities to report. The process is to include such items as: a vulnerability handling policy that identifies reporting process, responsible party for handling vulnerability reports, and a process for intake, assignment, remediation, and remediation testing. As part of the process, use a vulnerability tracking system that includes severity ratings and metrics for measuring timing for identification, analysis, and remediation of vulnerabilities. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard. Third-party application developers need to consider this an externally-facing policy that helps to set expectations for outside stakeholders.

compliance.control: Establish and maintain a process to accept and address reports of software vulnerabilities, including providing a means for external entities to report. The process is to include such items as: a vulnerability handling policy that identifies reporting process, responsible party for handling vulnerability reports, and a process for intake, assignment, remediation, and remediation testing. As part of the process, use a vulnerability tracking system that includes severity ratings and metrics for measuring timing for identification, analysis, and remediation of vulnerabilities. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard. Third-party application developers need to consider this an externally-facing policy that helps to set expectations for outside stakeholders.

Review and update the system and process annually.

compliance.control: Establish and maintain a severity rating system and process for application vulnerabilities that facilitates prioritizing the order in which discovered vulnerabilities are fixed. compliance.control: Use standard, industry-recommended hardening configuration templates for application infrastructure components. compliance.control: Ensure that all software development personnel receive training in writing secure code for their specific development environment and responsibilities. finding_info.desc: Conduct application penetration testing. For critical applications, authenticated penetration testing is better suited to finding business logic vulnerabilities than code scanning and automated security testing. Penetration testing relies on the skill of the tester to manually manipulate an application as an authenticated and unauthenticated user.

compliance.control: Conduct application penetration testing. finding_info.desc: Conduct threat modeling. Threat modeling is the process of identifying and addressing application security design flaws within a design, before code is created. It is conducted through specially trained individuals who evaluate the application design and gauge security risks for each entry point and access level. The goal is to map out the application, architecture, and infrastructure in a structured way to understand its weaknesses.

compliance.control: Conduct threat modeling. Threat modeling is the process of identifying and addressing application security design flaws within a design, before code is created. If using a service provider, designate at least one person internal to the enterprise to oversee any third-party work. compliance.control: Designate one key person, and at least one backup, who will manage the enterprise's incident handling process. compliance.control: Establish and maintain an documented enterprise process for the workforce to report security incidents. compliance.control: Assign key roles and responsibilities for incident response, including staff from legal, IT, information security, facilities, public relations, human resources, incident responders, analysts, and relevant third parties. compliance.control: Determine which primary and secondary mechanisms will be used to communicate and report during a security incident. finding_info.desc: Plan and conduct routine incident response exercises and scenarios for key personnel involved in the incident response process to prepare for responding to real-world incidents. Exercises need to test communication channels, decision making, and workflows. Conduct testing on an annual basis, at a minimum.

finding_info.desc: Conduct post-incident reviews. Post-incident reviews help prevent incident recurrence through identifying lessons learned and follow-up action.

finding_info.desc: Establish and maintain security incident thresholds, including, at a minimum, differentiating between an incident and an event. Examples can include: abnormal activity, security vulnerability, security weakness, data breach, privacy incident, etc. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

finding_info.desc: Establish and maintain a penetration testing program appropriate to the size, complexity, industry, and maturity of the enterprise. Penetration testing program characteristics include scope, such as network, web application, Application Programming Interface (API), hosted services, and physical premise controls; frequency; limitations, such as acceptable hours, and excluded attack types; point of contact information; remediation, such as how findings will be routed internally; and retrospective requirements.

compliance.control: Establish and maintain a penetration testing program appropriate to the size, complexity, industry, and maturity of the enterprise. Penetration testing program characteristics include scope, such as network, web application, Application Programming Interface (API), hosted services, and physical premise controls; frequency; limitations, such as acceptable hours, and excluded attack types; point of contact information; remediation, such as how findings will be routed internally; and retrospective requirements.

message: Manual check
status_detail: Manual check

finding_info.desc: Perform periodic external penetration tests based on program requirements, no less than annually. External penetration testing must include enterprise and environmental reconnaissance to detect exploitable information. Penetration testing requires specialized skills and experience and must be conducted through a qualified party. The testing may be clear box or opaque box.

compliance.control: Perform periodic external penetration tests based on program requirements, no less than annually. finding_info.desc: Remediate penetration test findings based on the enterprise's documented vulnerability remediation process. This should include determining a timeline and level of effort based on the impact and prioritization of each identified finding.
